what: Identify the top-cited papers across multiple articles
title: Annotation Workflow
text: get_top_cited_wiki_papers() combines EuropePMC annotation CrossRef citation counts rank impactful papers cited across article set.
code: articles  <- c("Zeitgeber", "Advanced sleep phase disorder", "Sleep deprivation") recent_all <- get_category_articles_most_recent(articles) doi_all    <- get_regex_citations_in_wiki_table(recent_all, pkg.env$doi_regexp)  top40 <- get_top_cited_wiki_papers(doi_all) head(top40[, c("title", "journalTitle", "pubYear",                "wiki_count", "count", "cited_in_wiki_art")]) #>                                                                                                                                                    title #> 1                                               Short sleep duration is associated with reduced leptin, elevated ghrelin, and increased body mass index. #> 2 Should I study or should I go (to sleep)? title: Citation Analysis with wikilite
text: vignette demonstrates extract, count, parse, visualise citations Wikipedia articles using wikilite. work small set articles sleep science illustrate step.

title: Citation Analysis with wikilite
code: library(wikilite)  articles <- c("Zeitgeber",               "Advanced sleep phase disorder",               "Sleep deprivation",               "Circadian rhythm")  # Most recent revision of each article recent <- get_category_articles_most_recent(articles)

title: Citation Analysis with wikilite
text: package stores built-regular expressions pkg.env environment. can inspect : Apply single pattern: Apply every pattern receive named list:

title: Citation Analysis with wikilite
code: results <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(recent)), function(i) {   text <- recent$`*`[i]   data.frame(     art          = recent$art[i],     n_doi        = get_doi_count(text),     n_isbn       = get_ISBN_count(text),     n_url        = get_urlCount(text),     n_ref        = get_refCount(text),     n_hyperlinks = get_hyperlinkCount(text),     sci_score    = get_sci_score(text)   ) })  count_table <- do.call(rbind, results) count_table #>                             art n_doi n_isbn n_url n_ref n_hyperlinks sci_score #> 1                     Zeitgeber    13      0     3    16           33 1.0000000 #> 2 Advanced sleep phase disorder    19      0     3    20           52 0.9500000 #> 3             Sleep deprivation   136     40   150   241          245 0.5756303 #> 4              Circadian rhythm   144     12    41   165          270 0.8546512

title: Citation Analysis with wikilite
code: # Top 20 journal titles across all articles plot_top_source(parsed, "journal") # Top 20 publishers plot_top_source(parsed, "publisher") # Generate charts for multiple fields at once get_pdfs_top20source(parsed,   source_types_list = c("journal", "publisher", "author"))

title: Citation Analysis with wikilite
text: SciScore measures proportion CS1 citations use cite journal template — proxy scientifically sourced article .
code: scores <- sapply(seq_len(nrow(recent)), function(i) {   get_sci_score(recent$`*`[i]) }) names(scores) <- recent$art sort(scores, decreasing = TRUE) #>                     Zeitgeber Advanced sleep phase disorder  #>                     1.0000000                     0.9500000  #>              Circadian rhythm             Sleep deprivation  #>                     0.8546512                     0.5756303

title: Citation Analysis with wikilite
text: get_top_cited_wiki_papers() finds 40 frequently cited DOIs across supplied articles, annotates via EuropePMC CrossRef, adds per-article citation counts.
code: doi_df <- get_regex_citations_in_wiki_table(recent, pkg.env$doi_regexp) top_papers <- get_top_cited_wiki_papers(doi_df) head(top_papers[, c("title", "journalTitle", "pubYear",                     "wiki_count", "cited_in_wiki_art")]) #>                                                                                                                                                               title #> 1                                              Contrasting Effects of Sleep Restriction, Total Sleep Deprivation, and Sleep Timing on Positive and Negative Affect. #> 2      Rapid Eye Movement Sleep Deprivation Produces Long-Term Detrimental Effects in Spatial Memory and Modifies the Cellular Composition of the Subgranular Zone. #> 3 Rapid Eye Movement Sleep Deprivation Induces Neuronal Apoptosis by Noradrenaline Acting on Alpha1 Adrenoceptor and by Triggering Mitochondrial Intrinsic Pathway. #> 4                                                  Relationship between shift work, night work, and subsequent dementia: A systematic evaluation and meta-analysis. #> 5                                                                Effects of Chronic Sleep Restriction on the Brain Functional Network, as Revealed by Graph Theory. #> 6                                                                                     Predicting and mitigating fatigue effects due to sleep deprivation: A review. #>           journalTitle pubYear wiki_count cited_in_wiki_art #> 1 Front Behav Neurosci    2022          1 Sleep deprivation #> 2  Front Cell Neurosci    2016          1 Sleep deprivation #> 3         Front Neurol    2016          1 Sleep deprivation #> 4         Front Neurol    2022          1  Circadian rhythm #> 5       Front Neurosci    2019          1 Sleep deprivation #> 6       Front Neurosci    2022          1 Sleep deprivation

title: Citation Analysis with wikilite
text: longitudinal analysis, retrieve full revision history:
code: history <- get_article_full_history_table("Zeitgeber",                                           date_an = "2022-01-01T00:00:00Z")  # Extract DOIs from every revision and track when they first appeared doi_history <- get_regex_citations_in_wiki_table(history, pkg.env$doi_regexp)  # How many unique DOIs appear by revision? length(unique(doi_history$citation_fetched)) #> [1] 14

title: Getting Started with wikilite
code: # CRAN (once available): install.packages("wikilite")  # Development version from GitHub: remotes::install_github("jsobel1/wikilite")

title: Getting Started with wikilite
text: basic workflow fetches recent revision article computes quality metrics. Results cached disk default — subsequent calls arguments return instantly.
code: library(wikilite)  # Fetch the most recent revision of Zeitgeber art <- get_article_most_recent_table("Zeitgeber")  # SciScore: proportion of journal citations (0–1) get_sci_score(art$`*`) #> [1] 1  # Raw counts get_doi_count(art$`*`) #> [1] 13 get_refCount(art$`*`) #> [1] 16

title: Getting Started with wikilite
code: # Get all article titles in a Wikipedia category articles <- get_pagename_in_cat("Circadian rhythm")  # Fetch most recent revisions for all articles recent <- get_category_articles_most_recent(articles)  # Or fetch in parallel with 4 workers (requires furrr + future) history <- get_category_articles_history(articles, workers = 4L)

title: Getting Started with wikilite
text: API functions accept lang parameter:
code: # French Wikipedia fr_art <- get_article_most_recent_table("COVID-19", lang = "fr")  # German Wikipedia category de_articles <- get_pagename_in_cat("Chronobiologie", lang = "de")

code: library(wikilite)  # Most recent revision of the Zeitgeber article recent <- get_article_most_recent_table("Zeitgeber")  # The data frame has columns: art, revid, user, timestamp, size, comment, * # The "*" column contains the full raw wikitext. names(recent) #> [1] "art"       "revid"     "parentid"  "user"      "userid"    "timestamp" #> [7] "size"      "comment"   "*" text <- recent$`*`  get_doi_count(text)        # number of DOIs #> [1] 13 get_refCount(text)         # number of <ref>...</ref> blocks #> [1] 16 get_ISBN_count(text)       # number of ISBNs #> [1] 0 get_hyperlinkCount(text)   # number of [[...]] links #> [1] 33 get_sci_score(text)        # fraction of citations that are journal citations #> [1] 1

Installation Quick start Retrieve article history Navigate categories Extract citations Parse Citation Style 1 templates Quality metrics — SciScore Annotate DOIs Annotate ISBNs Visualise Detect revert trends Interactive timeline networks Export results Function reference Built-regular expressions Data model Dependencies Contributing Citation License

title: wikilite — Wikipedia History & Citation Mining
text: Wikipedia world’s largest encyclopaedia primary reference point hundreds millions readers. Understanding cites scientific literature — papers referenced, citations evolve time, scientifically sourced different topic areas — requires programmatic access revision history structured citation data. wikilite makes research straightforward: Pull complete edit history English Wikipedia article single function call. Extract every citation type (DOIs, ISBNs, PMIDs, URLs, CS1 templates) using battle-tested regular expressions patterns. Annotate extracted identifiers EuropePMC, CrossRef, Altmetric, Google Books, Open Library. Compute SciScore — reproducible metric scientifically sourced article . Identify revert-tagged edits detect disputed vandalism-prone articles. Generate ggplot2 visualisations — timelines, edit-activity charts, citation distributions — one line code. Build interactive htmlwidgets — Plotly timelines three types visNetwork graphs — directly list article titles.

title: wikilite — Wikipedia History & Citation Mining
text: System requirements: R ≥ 4.1.0 (native pipe operator |>). Java compiled-code dependencies.
code: # Stable release from CRAN install.packages("wikilite")  # Development version from GitHub # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("jsobel1/wikilite")

title: wikilite — Wikipedia History & Citation Mining
code: library(wikilite)  # 1. Fetch the most recent revision of an article recent <- get_article_most_recent_table("Zeitgeber")  # 2. Count citations in the raw wikitext text <- recent$`*` get_doi_count(text)        #> 12 get_refCount(text)         #> 38 get_sci_score(text)        #> 0.76  (76% of CS1 citations are journal citations)  # 3. Annotate the DOIs via EuropePMC doi_df   <- get_regex_citations_in_wiki_table(recent, pkg.env$doi_regexp) annotated <- annotate_doi_list_europmc(unique(doi_df$citation_fetched)) head(annotated[, c("title", "journalTitle", "pubYear", "citedByCount")])

what: wikilite: Retrieve, Parse, and Analyse Wikipedia Article History and Citations — wikilite-package
title: wikilite: Retrieve, Parse, and Analyse Wikipedia Article History and Citations — wikilite-package
text: **wikilite** toolkit mining Wikipedia article revision history citations via public MediaWiki API.  Core capabilities: History retrieval Full partial revision histories one     many articles via get_article_full_history_table,     get_article_initial_table,     get_article_most_recent_table. Category navigation get_pagename_in_cat,     get_subcat_table,     get_subcat_with_depth related helpers. Citation extraction Built-regular expressions DOIs, ISBNs,     PMIDs, URLs, Citation Style 1 templates, accessible via     get_regex_citations_in_wiki_table     extract_citations_regexp. Citation parsing parse_article_ALL_citations parses     every CS1 template tidy long data frame; counting helpers     (get_doi_count, get_refCount, …) provide     fast scalar summaries. Quality metrics get_sci_score (proportion     journal citations). Annotation annotate_doi_list_europmc,     annotate_doi_list_cross_ref,     annotate_doi_list_altmetrics,     annotate_isbn_google,     annotate_isbn_openlib. Revert trends get_revert_counts retrieves     count revert-tagged edits across Wikipedia articles     given time window. Visualisation Timelines, edit-activity plots, citation     distribution charts built ggplot2.

what: New features: interactive timelines and networks
title: wikilite 0.2.0
text: Four new exported functions powered plotly visNetwork: plot_interactive_timeline(articles, date_an, color_by) — Plotly Gantt-style timeline showing article creation last-edit dates. Bars colour-coded SciScore (default), article size, uniform colour. Hover text includes creation date, first editor, byte sizes. Clicking labels opens Wikipedia article. plot_article_publication_network(articles, date_an, top_n_dois, min_wiki_count, annotate) — Bipartite visNetwork article nodes (blue squares) publication nodes (orange circles). Edge direction: article → cited DOI. Node size reflects citation degree. annotate = TRUE, publication labels enriched EuropePMC paper titles. Clicking node opens corresponding Wikipedia DOI URL. plot_article_cocitation_network(articles, date_an, min_shared_dois) — Article–article visNetwork edges represent shared DOI citations. Edge thickness scales number shared papers; hovering edge lists top shared DOIs. plot_article_wikilink_network(articles, date_an, only_internal, top_n_links) — Directed visNetwork [[...]]-style wikilinks. only_internal = TRUE (default) shows links within supplied article set; only_internal = FALSE adds top external link targets. Node size reflects -degree. Three unexported helper functions (*.build_pubnet_data, .build_cocite_data, .build_wikilink_data) separate data construction rendering enable unit-testing without network connection.

title: wikilite 0.2.0
text: Added plotly Imports (interactive timeline). Added visNetwork Imports (three network functions).

title: wikilite 0.1.0
text: Renamed Get_sci_score() → get_sci_score(), Get_sci_score2() → get_sci_score2(), Get_source_type_counts() → get_source_type_counts(), get_paresd_citations() → get_parsed_citations() consistent snake_case naming. Replaced archived xlsx / rJava dependency openxlsx (pure R, cross-platform). Removed unused archived packages: timevis, textreuse, ggridges, gridExtra, scales, curl, XML. plot_navi_timeline() now issues informative message instead failing silently timevis unavailable. examples require network connection wrapped \dontrun{}. Comprehensive testthat edition 3 test suite (network tests guarded skip_on_cran()). Three vignettes covering introduction, citation analysis, annotation workflows.
